what_it_does: Defines a user-level systemd target unit named company.target that serves as a single start point for a set of local services (canvas, bridge, brain, voice).
observations: It is a systemd [Unit]/[Install] target file, not a service file.
Comments identify it as the single place everything starts via systemctl --user start company.target.
Membership is achieved through each service's WantedBy=company.target or via add-wants for existing units, rather than a Wants= list in this file.
It is enabled into default.target via [Install].
Documentation points to a local file path under /home/tim.
standouts: Acts as the root startup anchor for four named local services.
Uses reverse-dependency wiring (WantedBy=company.target) instead of listing members explicitly.
Installed as a user unit into default.target.
conventions: Follows systemd unit file section structure ([Unit], [Install]).
Uses Description, Documentation, After, and WantedBy fields idiomatically.
Uses inline comments to document intended usage and membership policy.
concerns: Documentation path is hardcoded to /home/tim, which may not be portable.
The target itself does not explicitly name its members, so membership is scattered across other units or external add-wants commands.
The comment claims it is the single place everything starts, but actual startup orchestration depends on other files not shown here.
notes: This is the top-level user systemd target that bootstraps the local service suite by letting member units pull themselves into it.
questions: Which exact unit files declare WantedBy=company.target?
Where and how are add-wants commands recorded or executed?
Is /home/tim/company/ops/STARTUP.md the canonical documentation path on all hosts?
Are canvas, bridge, brain, and voice user units or system units?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose mostly matches: it declares a target for all local services and a single start point, but the file itself does not enumerate members, leaving membership to other units or external add-wants commands.
intention_signals: It appears designed to create a one-command startup ritual for a personal/local service ecosystem, using a named systemd target as a stable anchor while letting individual services opt in via reverse dependencies.
novelty: Defines a project-named user target as the root for a small named suite of local services, relying on reverse WantedBy rather than an explicit Wants= list.
contribution: Provides a stable, named aggregation point and startup ritual for the local service set; records the intended member services and a documentation pointer; enables downstream units to declare membership by wiring themselves into this target.
summary_for_embedding: A user-level systemd target unit that groups the local services canvas, bridge, brain, and voice under a single start point with a network dependency and default.target enablement.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a one-command startup anchor for the local service suite by letting member units pull themselves into a shared target.
